title: Color and contrast

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Color and contrast; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Color and contrast
sources.claim: Text and meaningful graphical objects need contrast against their actual backgrounds across default, hover, focus, disabled, and theme states.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Color and contrast
sources.claim: Text and meaningful graphical objects need contrast against their actual backgrounds across default, hover, focus, disabled, and theme states.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Text and meaningful graphical objects need contrast against their actual backgrounds across default, hover, focus, disabled, and theme states.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A focus indicator and error text are measured on every background they can overlap, not only the design canvas. Your goal is to measure and repair text, component and focus-indicator contrast. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Lengths, percentages and viewport units; html semantics text; Custom properties and design tokens; Readable text metrics. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Text and meaningful graphical objects need contrast against their actual backgrounds across default, hover, focus, disabled, and theme states. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Transparency, gradients, images, thin fonts, antialiasing, and user themes can change effective contrast. This draft therefore rejects: A contrast-safe text hex value remains compliant on every translucent or image background.

includes: Choose foreground, background, focus and state colors that satisfy contrast and do not encode meaning alone.
Measure and repair text, component and focus-indicator contrast.
excludes: Transparency, gradients, images, thin fonts, antialiasing, and user themes can change effective contrast.

outcomes: Measure and repair text, component and focus-indicator contrast.

examples.title: Color and contrast: concrete case
examples.situation: A focus indicator and error text are measured on every background they can overlap, not only the design canvas.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A focus indicator and error text are measured on every background they can overlap, not only the design canvas.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Measure the rendered foreground-background pair in each state; a color value alone has no contrast ratio.
examples.result: The result follows because Measure the rendered foreground-background pair in each state; a color value alone has no contrast ratio.

examples.title: Color and contrast: boundary case
examples.situation: Transparency, gradients, images, thin fonts, antialiasing, and user themes can change effective contrast.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Transparency, gradients, images, thin fonts, antialiasing, and user themes can change effective contrast.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: A contrast-safe text hex value remains compliant on every translucent or image background.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Measure the rendered foreground-background pair in each state; a color value alone has no contrast ratio.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: A contrast-safe text hex value remains compliant on every translucent or image background.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Color and contrast, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Color and contrast before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.

recap.summary: You should now be able to measure and repair text, component and focus-indicator contrast. Keep this boundary in view: Transparency, gradients, images, thin fonts, antialiasing, and user themes can change effective contrast.

Why is this claim incorrect: A contrast-safe text hex value remains compliant on every translucent or image background.
Apply checkpoint 'foreground-background pair' to a new artifact.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
